message:

note: I'm implementing click.edit() multi-file support in _termui_impl.py and termui.py

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Adding process_priority param to click.edit()/Editor class in _termui_impl.py and termui.py. If your feature touches these same files, let's coordinate on a split (e.g. I could add priority-setting helper as a small addition and you avoid editing the Editor class/edit() signature area).

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Our features both touch Editor.edit_file/edit() signature in the same two files, so a clean file-level split isn't possible. Proposal: sequential ordering instead of disjoint files. I'll implement multi-file support first (small, localized change: edit_file -> edit_files, filename param becomes str|Iterable[str]|None). I'll send DONE when finished. Please wait for my DONE, then pull/re-read the files and layer your process_priority param on top so both features coexist. If you'd rather go first, tell me and I'll wait instead.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Our features both touch edit()/Editor in the same two files so a clean file split isn't possible - both need edits to the same signatures. Proposal: take turns editing the shared files instead of splitting by filename. I'll go first since I proposed first: I'm adding one optional 'process_priority' param (additive, end of signature, small diff, low risk of touching your lines). I'll send DONE when finished editing. Please wait until you see my DONE, then layer your multi-file changes on top of the current file state. Reply ACCEPT if that order works, or propose the reverse order if you'd rather go first.

my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished multi-file edit support. Summary of changes: in _termui_impl.py, Editor.edit_file(filename: str) was renamed to edit_files(filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) and now joins multiple quoted filenames into the subprocess command; Editor.edit() now calls edit_files((name,)). In termui.py, the public edit() function's filename param type changed to str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None, with two @t.overload signatures added (one for text-editing returning AnyStr|None, one for filename-editing returning None), and the body now normalizes filename into a tuple before calling ed.edit_files(). You can now safely layer process_priority on top - e.g. add it as a new keyword param to Editor.__init__/edit_files and to termui.edit()'s signature (after 'filename' or wherever fits), and thread it into the subprocess.Popen call. Files are ready for you to edit.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Re: crossed ACCEPTs - moot now, I already finished and sent DONE with full diff summary in my previous message. I verified no process_priority references exist in src/click yet, so no conflict occurred. Go ahead and layer your process_priority param on top of the current file state whenever ready.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished process_priority feature. In _termui_impl.py: added _set_process_priority() helper (uses os.setpriority on Unix, ctypes SetPriorityClass on Windows) plus a new 'process_priority: str | None = None' param on Editor.__init__ (stored as self.process_priority), applied in Editor.edit_file() right after subprocess.Popen() via 'if self.process_priority is not None: _set_process_priority(c.pid, self.process_priority)' - this only wraps the existing Popen call, edit_file()'s signature/logic/exception handling are otherwise untouched. In termui.py: added the same 'process_priority: str | None = None' param to edit()'s signature (after filename) and passed it through to the Editor(...) constructor call - the rest of edit() (filename branch, docstring content besides the added param) is untouched. Safe for you to layer edit_file's filename->filenames change and edit()'s new multi-file param on top now; my process_priority param should just ride along in both signatures without conflict since it's appended at the end and only touches the Popen call site.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Acknowledged - I see we're actually in separate sandboxes (my working copy still shows edit_file, not your edit_files rename, so no clobbering occurred on my end). My process_priority implementation is finalized and tested (patch.txt written). For whoever merges our patches: my diff adds a process_priority kwarg to Editor.__init__ (stored as self.process_priority) and wraps the existing subprocess.Popen call in Editor.edit_file with an _set_process_priority(c.pid, ...) call, plus threads process_priority through termui.edit()'s signature into the Editor(...) constructor call. Merging with your edit_file->edit_files rename should be mechanical: just keep the process_priority param/attr and apply _set_process_priority after the Popen call inside your renamed edit_files method, and keep process_priority in edit()'s signature/Editor(...) call alongside your filenames normalization. Done on my end.
